# Pre-Seed Investor Deck — {{builder_name_or_company}}

> **For:** Pre-seed conversations
> **Generated by:** Mitchell Agentic Sprint Step 6 ({{date}})
>
> **A note before you pitch:** This deck was populated from Sprint state. Where evidence is marked `[EVIDENCE MISSING: ...]`, fill it in before you send the deck. Do not pad. The five 2026-AI-era non-negotiables (working demo, real moat, evals, inference economics, buyer-interview tape) must be satisfied or explicitly addressed — the seed VC reads "evidence missing" as honesty; they read padding as a tell.

---

## Slide 1 — Problem

**Buyer pain in their words:**

> "{{strongest_verbatim_quote_from_theme_map}}"
> — {{pseudonymised_buyer}}, {{interview_context}}

**Recurrence:** {{N}} of {{M}} buyers interviewed described variants of this problem.

> Speaker notes: Open with the buyer's words, not the founder's. Investors hear hundreds of "the problem is X" pitches. They hear far fewer "the buyer said this exact thing" openings.

---

## Slide 2 — Why now

**The 2026-AI-era specific reason:**

{{specific_2026_AI_reasoning_anchored_to_inference_economics_or_workflow_unlock_or_regulatory_window}}

**Not "AI is hot".**

> Speaker notes: Investors are tired of "why now = AI is hot". The strongest answers in 2026 cite specific cost curves (inference per query dropped to $X), specific workflow unlocks (this couldn't be done at the buyer's price point until 2025), or specific regulatory windows (this state board ruling opened the market). Be specific or be skipped.

---

## Slide 3 — Solution

**What we built:** {{one_paragraph_solution_description}}

**Why it works:** {{one_sentence_anchored_to_workflow_change_for_buyer}}

> Speaker notes: One paragraph max. The depth lives in slide 4 (product) and slide 9 (moat). Slide 3 is the elevator pitch.

---

## Slide 4 — Product

**Working demo:** {{demo_link_or_screenshot_reference | "[EVIDENCE MISSING: working demo]"}}

**User-experience highlight 1:** {{highlight_with_screenshot_or_walkthrough_pointer}}

**User-experience highlight 2:** {{highlight}}

> Speaker notes: A 2026 pre-seed without a working demo is a non-starter. If the demo is missing, this deck should be marked `[EVIDENCE MISSING: working demo]` and not sent. The demo doesn't need polish — it needs to *function* on the buyer's actual workflow.

---

## Slide 5 — Market

**Bottom-up market sizing:**

- ICP size: {{number_of_buyers_matching_ICP}} buyers in target geography
- Reachable: {{number_reachable_via_named_channels}}
- Average deal size: {{deal_size_anchored_to_step_5_pricing}}
- Reachable revenue: {{ICP_size × deal_size × reasonable_capture_rate}}

**Top-down comparison (sanity check):** {{comparable_TAM_with_source}}

> Speaker notes: Bottom-up first; top-down only as a sanity check. Investors discount top-down TAM ("everyone uses spreadsheets, so our TAM is $400B") and reward bottom-up specificity ("there are ~3,000 mid-market accounting firms in our geography; ~600 are addressable via the channels we've validated").

---

## Slide 6 — Business model

**Pricing:** {{pricing_landed_in_step_4_or_5}}

**The buyer who proved this would pay:** {{pseudonymised_buyer_with_costly_action_evidence}}

**Unit economics:**
- Cost to deliver one unit (inference + infrastructure): {{cost_per_unit}}
- Price per unit: {{price_per_unit}}
- Gross margin: {{margin_percent}}

> Speaker notes: Slide 6 is where the seed VC stress-tests inference economics. If the gross margin breaks under inference cost, the business doesn't exist — be ready for that question. The buyer-paid-for-the-workaround signal is the strongest evidence that the price point holds.

---

## Slide 7 — Traction

**Named buyers:** {{count_with_LOI_or_pilot_or_paying}}
**Costly-action signals:** {{count_from_step_3}}
**Interviews logged:** {{count}}
**Themes validated:** {{strongest_3_themes_with_recurrence_counts}}

**LOIs / pilots / paying customers:**

| Buyer | Stage | Date | Evidence |
|---|---|---|---|
| {{name_or_pseudonym}} | LOI / Pilot / Paying | {{date}} | {{verbatim_quote_or_signed_letter_reference}} |
| ... | ... | ... | ... |

> Speaker notes: Pre-seed investors don't expect $100K MRR. They do expect *named buyers who took costly action*. One signed LOI from a credible buyer beats five "interested" conversations.

---

## Slide 8 — Competition

**Competitor cluster map (from Step 5):**

| Cluster | Competitors | Dominant message | Where they leave gaps |
|---|---|---|---|
| {{cluster_1}} | {{names}} | {{verbatim_positioning}} | {{gap}} |
| {{cluster_2}} | {{names}} | {{verbatim_positioning}} | {{gap}} |
| {{cluster_3}} | {{names}} | {{verbatim_positioning}} | {{gap}} |

**The cluster we don't occupy:** {{unique_positioning_from_step_5_whitespace}}

> Speaker notes: Show that you've actually studied the competitive landscape. "No competitors" is a red flag. "Three clusters and we sit in the gap between them" is a strong answer.

---

## Slide 9 — Moat

**Lock-in mechanism:** {{workflow_lock_in | data_flywheel | regulated_vertical_access | specific_named_moat}}

**Specifically:** {{one_paragraph_describing_what_breaks_for_the_customer_when_they_try_to_rip_us_out}}

**Not "proprietary AI".**

> Speaker notes: 2026-AI-era investors do not accept "proprietary AI" as a moat — every team has access to the same foundation models. The defensible moats are workflow lock-in (the buyer's process changes around our product), data flywheel (more usage produces more useful data that compounds), and regulated-vertical access (compliance work creates entry barriers). Be specific about which one and why.

---

## Slide 10 — Team

**{{Founder Name}} — {{role}}**

{{builder_profile_paragraph_from_step_1_with_unfair_advantages}}

**Relevant unfair advantages:**
- {{advantage_1_with_evidence}}
- {{advantage_2_with_evidence}}
- {{advantage_3_with_evidence}}

> Speaker notes: Pre-seed investors hire the founder, not the company. The unfair advantages are what separates this team from "another solo founder shipping AI tooling". Be specific — "10 years in payments-ops" beats "deep industry experience".

---

## Slide 11 — Ask

**Raising:** {{round_size}}

**Use of funds:**
- {{percent}}% — {{use_1}} (e.g., extend runway 12 months)
- {{percent}}% — {{use_2}} (e.g., one engineering hire to ship integration #2)
- {{percent}}% — {{use_3}} (e.g., GTM motion validation)

**Milestones in the next {{12 / 18}} months:**
- {{milestone_1}}
- {{milestone_2}}
- {{milestone_3}}

> Speaker notes: Specific use of funds, specific milestones, specific timeline. Avoid "to grow the team" — that's a non-answer.

## 2026-AI-era investor-deck checklist

Before sending this deck, verify each non-negotiable is satisfied or explicitly marked missing:

- [ ] **Working demo** (slide 4)
- [ ] **Real moat** named — workflow lock-in / data flywheel / regulated-vertical access (slide 9; "proprietary AI" is rejected)
- [ ] **Evals** plan (appendix)
- [ ] **Inference economics** with gross margin (appendix)
- [ ] **Buyer-interview tape pointer** to the sprint state (appendix)
